publishedAbstract: It has been previously shown in the [Quay Documentation](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_quay/3.7/html/deploy_red_hat_quay_on_openshift_with_the_quay_operator/operator-preconfigure#create_a_standalone_object_gateway) to deploy a NooBaa MCG stack using a NooBaa CR yaml instead of a storagecluster.yaml or going through the ODF UI to create the standalone MCG. This is no longer supported and has been shown to cause issues during the ODF v4.18- ODF v4.19 upgrade, as demonstrated in the [Standalone MCG Deployed via Quay docs fails ODF upgrade to 4.19](https://access.redhat.com/articles/7134589) solution. If this configuration is discovered and can be easily identified with an empty storagecluster.yaml in the `openshift-storage` namespace, it will be required to migrate this configuration before the upgrade to ODF v4.19. **RELATED ARTICLES:** [Standalone MCG Deployed via Quay Documentation Fails ODF v4.19 Upgrade](https://access.redhat.com/articles/7134589)
publishedTitle: Migrating from a Quay Standalone Multicloud Object Gateway (MCG) to an ODF StorageCluster-Managed MCG - NooBaa
